title: vexatorella obtusata subsp. obtusata
intro: Vexatorella obtusata subsp. obtusata, also known as the 'Mountain Devil', is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. It is native to Australia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with the roots and leaves possessing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Vexatorella obtusata subsp. obtusata include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the roots and leaves, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
